description_long: This is a single-story wooden cabin with a prominent gable roof covered in reddish-brown shingles. The structure is constructed from light-colored timber logs, with visible interlocking corners and a raised foundation supported by concrete blocks. The front facade features a partially open porch area with a small set of steps leading up to it, and the main entrance is located to the right of the porch. Large rectangular windows with multiple panes are present on the right side, providing natural light to the interior. The rear of the cabin is solid, with no windows or doors, while the left side mirrors the right but with fewer windows. The overall design is simple and functional, evoking a rustic, log cabin aesthetic suitable for residential or recreational use.
description: A rustic wooden cabin with a gable roof, open porch, and multi-pane windows, built from light timber logs and raised on concrete blocks.
description_view_0: The front view shows the gable roof, open porch with steps, and the main entrance on the right side.
description_view_1: The right side view highlights the large multi-pane window and the side of the porch, with the entrance visible.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a solid wooden wall with no windows or doors, emphasizing the cabin's enclosed back.
description_view_3: The left side view features a single window and the open porch area, mirroring the right side but with fewer windows.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with moderate overhangs, covered in reddish-brown asphalt shingles
attribute_window_arrangement: Asymmetrical arrangement with one large multi-pane rectangular window on the right side and a single smaller window on the left; no windows on the rear or front facades.
attribute_door_placement: Single exterior door located on the front facade, offset to the right and adjacent to the porch steps.
